text:
This paper reveals Yudian Wahyudi’s idea of *Maqasid al-Shari’ah* in tackling various problems faced by the Indonesian nation in the present era. Based on his works and works of other related authors, the researcher found that the theory of Maqasid al-Shari’ah of Yudian Wahyudi has led to the emergence of an “Indonesian Maqasid” theory, which is different from the classical and modern views. This theory attempts to connect the traditional five basic needs (*daruriyyat al-khams*) with the ideological values of Pancasila which is the state ideology of Indonesia.

Several fundamentals may be enumerated as forming the theoretical basis of his work: *tauhidul ‘ulum*, with its integrative and unifying of the various aspects of existence — Quranic, cosmic and human, comprising *daruriyyat al-thalathah* — in order to provide a contemporary substance for the call to return to the Quran and the Sunnah; the theory of the coupling of two fundamental principles in the Islamic shari‘a — the divine and the human, the universal and the particular, the eternal and the temporal; and the use of *Maqasid* as a prismatic lens in order to make a detailed analysis of society; all of these within the general context of Indonesian society.

Indonesian Maqasid offers a rare attempt to bring the Islamic Objectives in line with the five principles of Pancasila — faith (*hifz al-din*), human life (*hifz al-nafs*), the family (*hifz al-nasl*), rational thinking (*hifz al-’aql*) and social justice (*hifz al-mal*). Based on an independent assessment this attempt is no longer a mere theoretical exercise but results in a unique synthesis that serves to further enhance *Usul al-Fiqh* and to arrive at a more authentic *Maqasid al-Shariah* in accordance with the Indonesian socio-political context.
